title: Stelis pidax
taxonomy: Stelis pidax is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. The genus Stelis is a group of orchids that are primarily epiphytes, with a wide range of species found in tropical regions. Stelis pidax is a specific species within this genus, known for its unique characteristics and growth habits. The classification of Stelis pidax reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchids and its place within the broader family of flowering plants. This classification is based on a combination of morphological, anatomical, and molecular characteristics.

intro: Stelis pidax, also known as Hoary Stelis, is a type of orchid plant species native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Stelis pidax has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including coughs and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the throat and lungs. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis pidax is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs in cough syrups and respiratory remedies.
